ERP for Infrastructure Projects

Infrastructure
ERP Software

SiteFlow connects field teams and back offices in one infrastructure ERP platform. Monitor chainage-wise progress, track equipment, control costs, and keep projects on schedule — from any device on site.

Header
The Problem

Why Infrastructure Projects Go Over Budget

Every infra contractor faces the same problems. Most accept them as normal. They are not.

Card 1: Fake Attendance
📋Site Register — Day 14
Raju Kumar — Mason✕ Off-site
Suresh Helper✕ Not verified
Ahmed Painter△ Marked absent
Fake Attendance

Supervisors mark registers manually across chainages and camps. Workers who were never on site get counted. Payroll calculations are wrong every month and disputes follow.

Card 2: Material Wastage COLOR: dot-p inline purple → rose already handled by .oifp-dot-p class
📦Material vs PO — Site A
TMT Bars
12 bundles short
Cement Bags
40 unmatched
Material Wastage

Materials are ordered over calls. No one checks delivery against the purchase order. Over-ordering and losses are discovered only after handover — when nothing can be recovered.

Card 3: Delayed DPR
📄DPR Status
Earthwork DPR — 3 days overdue
RCC Work DPR — not submitted
Formwork DPR — submitted today
Delayed Daily Reports

Site engineers send DPR updates over WhatsApp days after the work happens. By the time the office knows about a delay or cost issue, the damage is already done.

Card 4: Subcontractor Overbilling COLOR: dot-p on measured row → rose via class
💲Subcontractor Bill — Ramesh Civil
Claimed: 4,200 sqft
Unverified
Measured: 3,140 sqft
Actual
Subcontractor Overbilling

Subcontractors bill for more work than was actually executed. Without measured quantities linked to bills, there is no way to catch it before payment goes out.

Card 5: No Live P&L — full width COLOR: timeline bars #c4b5fd → #fda4b8 rose-light; #7c3aed → #d30d50
📈Project Cost — NH-48 Road Package
Overrun: ₹38L undetected
Apr
May
Jun
Jul
Aug
Sep
Oct
Nov
No Live Project P&L

Project cost is visible only after every bill is in — usually after handover. By then there is nothing to fix. A ₹38L overrun that built up over six months shows up as one number at the end, with no point in the timeline where action was possible.

Header
One Platform

One Platform Instead of Six.

Infrastructure sites run on WhatsApp forwards, Excel sheets, and manual registers. SiteFlow replaces all of them — and adds AI on top.

Section header
Platform Features

Everything Infra Teams Need. One Platform.

Project scheduling, resource control, financial oversight, and field connectivity — built for how infrastructure sites actually work.

ROW 1: Project Planning
Project Execution

End-to-End Project Planning and Scheduling

Manage every stage from DPR creation and BOQ management through to construction scheduling and task dependencies. Site engineers get their tasks on the app. The office sees what’s moving and what’s not — without calling anyone.

  • Chainage-wise activity scheduling
  • DPR creation and submission from mobile
  • Real-time delay alerts before they compound
See this in a demo →

ROW 2: Financial Control (flip)
Finance and Contracts

Integrated Financial and Contract Management

Multi-crore infra budgets are hard to track when your data lives across Excel sheets and Tally. SiteFlow pulls all of it together. Budget vs actual, vendor bill verification, milestone-based payments — visible in one place, updated as work happens on site.

  • Budget vs actual at chainage and package level
  • Vendor bill verified against measured work before payment
  • Tally and Zoho Books sync — no double entry
See this in a demo →

ROW 3: Resource and Material
Resource Management

Centralized Resource and Material Control

Track material procurement, deliveries, and stock levels across every site in one place. When cement arrives short or a batch gets diverted, SiteFlow flags it the same day. Not at handover. Getting this right saves more than most infra companies realize.

  • Delivery matched to purchase order before acceptance
  • Live inventory across multiple sites and camps
  • Equipment utilization tracked per chainage
See this in a demo →

ROW 4: Workforce (flip)
Workforce

GPS Attendance, Payroll and Field Connectivity

Infrastructure sites spread across multiple chainages make manual attendance genuinely impossible to get right. GPS punch-in and face recognition fix this. Wages calculate automatically on verified data. Site engineers submit daily reports from their phones, not from memory two days later.

  • GPS and face recognition attendance across all sites
  • Payroll auto-calculated on verified punch-in data
  • Web and mobile access for office and field teams
See this in a demo →

Right: review cards
S
SKC Infra
Road and Highway Construction · India
★★★★★
Finally know what is happening at every chainage without calling anyone
We were managing multiple road packages and spending most of the day following up with site engineers on WhatsApp for progress updates. With SiteFlow, each chainage section has its own activity tracking and the DPRs come in from site on time. Office knows where things stand every morning without anyone having to ask.
Chainage TrackingDPR ManagementSite Visibility
A
Alamdar Infrastructure
Infrastructure Contractor · Middle East
★★★★★
Payroll disputes dropped to almost zero after GPS attendance
Our sites are spread across remote locations and manual attendance was a constant problem. Workers disputed wages every month and supervisors had no way to prove the records were accurate. GPS punch-in through SiteFlow changed this completely. The data is there, wages calculate automatically, and disputes have nearly stopped. That alone made it worth switching.
GPS AttendancePayroll AutomationLabour Control
V
Vijay Infra
Road Construction · India
★★★★★
Caught two subcontractor overbills in the first month itself
We had subcontractors claiming work quantities that our engineers had not verified. Earlier there was no easy way to check before payment went out. SiteFlow made site measurement a mandatory step before bill approval. In the first month we caught two bills where the claimed quantity did not match what was actually done. That saved more than what we paid for the software for the whole year.
Bill VerificationCost ControlSubcon Management
M
MVB Constructions
Infrastructure and Civil · India
★★★★★
Material shortages stopped surprising us midway through work
The biggest problem on our sites was finding out material was short only after work had already started and progress was blocked. Now every delivery is checked against the purchase order in SiteFlow before it is accepted. Stock levels update in real time. We see potential shortages three or four days in advance and can act on it. That has reduced idle time from equipment and labour waiting for material significantly.
Material TrackingProcurement ControlEquipment Utilization
FAQ
FAQ
Questions infrastructure companies ask before starting
Yes. SiteFlow is built for chainage-based tracking. You can create work packages by km range, assign activities at each chainage, and see overall progress on a single dashboard. Road and highway contractors use it for packages ranging from 10 km to 200 km across multiple work fronts simultaneously.
Yes. The SiteFlow mobile app lets site engineers log daily progress, upload photos, record equipment usage, and submit DPRs directly from the field. The office sees it in real time. GPS attendance can also be punched in from the same app, so there is no separate system to manage for field teams.
Site engineers enter measured quantities against each work order before a bill can be approved. SiteFlow compares the vendor’s claimed quantity against the measured figure and flags any gap. Bills go through a multi-level approval before payment. This process has helped contractors catch overbilling worth lakhs that would otherwise have gone undetected.
Yes. Bills, expenses, and payments recorded in SiteFlow sync automatically to Tally or Zoho Books. No double entry, no manual reconciliation at month end. The integration is configured during onboarding and works across multiple company books if needed.
Yes. GPS attendance works offline and syncs when connectivity is restored, which matters on remote highway and infrastructure sites. Face recognition is also supported for biometric verification at labour camps. Payroll is calculated automatically on verified attendance data, so disputed wage calculations stop being a monthly problem.
Most companies go live within 3 days. Onboarding covers project structure setup, chainage mapping, team roles, and training for site engineers and project managers. A dedicated onboarding manager is assigned at no extra cost. Field teams typically need one walkthrough to start using the app independently.
Yes. Every expense — material, labour, equipment, subcontractor — flows into a live P&L the moment it is recorded. You can view it per package, per chainage section, or at the company level. There is no waiting for month-end. If a cost overrun is building, you see it while there is still time to act on it.
SiteFlow starts at Rs 12,000 per user per year on the Business plan, with a minimum of 3 users. The Business+ plan at Rs 15,000 per user per year adds advanced BOQ, procurement, equipment management, and payroll. Enterprise pricing with custom configurations is available for larger EPC and infrastructure companies. No credit card required to start.
CTA BAND
